element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic available from start; mastery required for all encounters
One-hit-kill mechanic establishes high stakes immediately

element_introduction_order.element: Power-ups (Sun Power, Crown Power, Cross Power, Tiger Power, Star Power)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Non-permanent buffs that enhance Diskarmor attacks and traversal

element_introduction_order.element: Screen bombs (destroy all enemies on screen)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Emergency relief mechanic for overwhelming situations
Constant pressure throughout; failure triggers fire monster
Escalating threat that accelerates if not avoided; forces speed mastery
Shifts spatial paradigm; spatial navigation required

element_introduction_order.element: Boss encounters (Dorago final boss, shadow versions in Ligar's Castle)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Culminates skill synthesis; final area features shadow-palette enemies
Rare high-risk/high-reward items for advanced players

typical_relief_triggers: Safe zone at end of each level (score tally)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Constant escalating pressure: time limit creates baseline tension, fire monster entrance amplifies urgency, one-hit-kill mechanic maintains high stakes throughout, safe zones provide brief psychological relief before next level

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduces core mechanics (Diskarmor, jump/duck/climb) with manageable enemy density; time limit present but generous
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Transition to overhead exploration areas; enemy patterns increase complexity; power-up reliance shifts strategy
difficulty_curve.late_game: Levels 21-27 prevent continue feature; forces skill mastery; Ligar's Castle features shadow-palette enemies (Dorago boss, robot, spider); final areas extremely difficult in European version (capped stat progression bug)
notable_spikes: Fire monster entrance during time pressure (every level)
Transition to overhead areas (spatial paradigm shift)

notable_level_patterns.description: 27 levels, each concluding with score tally safe zone providing psychological relief and checkpoint before escalation
Every level has time limit; exceeding triggers fire monster that grows faster the longer player evades
Levels 1-20 allow continue feature (pay-to-continue arcade mechanic); levels 21-27 force restart on game over
NES port incorporates overhead exploration areas alongside sidescroller sections; breaks horizontal-only pacing
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-to-late game areas (e.g., god rooms, Indora areas)

notable_level_patterns.description: Final area (Ligar's Castle) features shadow-palette enemies: Dorago boss plus robot/spider unique enemies
